mechanism_narrative: A persistently low federal funds rate relative to its 6-month trailing average signals accommodative monetary policy, which lowers the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days. This is a continuous always-on trend effect, not conditioned on any threshold crossing.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 1.82% as of t-1mo, well below historical tightening peaks
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level (or its trend) and forward 20d returns stays below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year walk-forward window, or returns following low-FEDFUNDS periods are not significantly higher than the unconditional mean (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A Sahm-rule-like threshold crossing, where UNRATE rises more than 0.3 percentage points above its 12-month trailing minimum, signals the onset of labor market deterioration that historically precedes recessions and triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as equity risk premia rise.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises more than 0.3pp above its trailing 12-month minimum
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.0% as of t-1mo, near recent lows
failure_condition: In more than 50% of historical instances where the Sahm-like trigger fires, forward 20d SPX returns are non-negative, or the signal's Rank IC falls below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Joint acceleration in RSAFS retail sales growth and HOUST housing starts indicates simultaneous strength in consumer spending and residential investment, two independent demand channels whose co-movement signals broad-based economic expansion. This combined signal produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon more reliably than either series alone, because it captures both durable and non-durable demand strength jointly.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 501971 million as of t-2mo and HOUST at 1350 thousand units as of t-2mo
failure_condition: The joint RSAFS+HOUST interaction term shows no incremental predictive power (Rank IC gain < 0.02) over the best single-series model across any 3-year rolling window, or forward 60d returns are not significantly higher when both series are jointly accelerating versus when only one is.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions, sustained INDPRO growth reflects capacity utilization gains that later translate into margin compression and inflationary pressure, causing equity markets to anticipate slowing forward earnings growth. In this regime, positive INDPRO momentum is associated with negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon, reversing the typical procyclical relationship seen in early expansions.
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with elevated capacity utilization
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO momentum in identified late-cycle windows is followed by non-negative forward 60d SPX returns in more than 55% of instances, or the direction flip relative to early-cycle regimes fails to replicate out-of-sample.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS exceeds the trailing 12-month CPIAUCSL-implied inflation rate by more than 1 percentage point (i.e., real policy rate turns meaningfully positive), monetary policy is restrictive enough to compress equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS minus trailing 12-month CPIAUCSL inflation rate exceeds 1.0 percentage point
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 1.82% and CPIAUCSL at 250.857 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Forward 20d SPX returns during periods where the real-rate trigger is active are not significantly lower than the unconditional baseline (p > 0.05) across a 5-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: A joint pattern of decelerating PAYEMS month-over-month growth combined with a rising UNRATE captures an early labor-market turning point that is not detectable from either series individually, because payroll deceleration alone can reflect noise while UNRATE alone lags. The co-occurrence signals imminent risk-off sentiment and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over a short 5-day horizon as markets react quickly to labor data releases.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 148912 thousand and UNRATE at 4.0% as of t-1mo
failure_condition: The joint PAYEMS-deceleration-plus-UNRATE-rise signal fails to produce negative mean forward 5d returns significantly different from zero (p > 0.05), or the interaction adds no Rank IC improvement over PAYEMS or UNRATE alone.

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month momentum in RSAFS retail sales reflects robust consumer spending that feeds directly into near-term corporate revenue expectations, producing a short-lived positive drift in S&P 500 returns over the following 5 trading days. This is a continuous, always-on trend-following effect with no activation threshold.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 501971 million dollars as of t-2mo
failure_condition: Rank IC between RSAFS month-over-month growth and forward 5d SPX returns stays below 0.02 over any rolling 2-year window, or positive RSAFS momentum periods do not show significantly higher mean 5d returns (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening regime (FEDFUNDS rising over the trailing 6 months), a simultaneous decline in INDPRO indicates that restrictive policy is beginning to bite into industrial output, foreshadowing an earnings recession. This joint condition produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon that are stronger than either signal alone would predict.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rising over trailing 6 months while INDPRO declines month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 107.3006 as of t-2mo and FEDFUNDS at 1.82% as of t-1mo
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with industrial slowdown
failure_condition: Instances where FEDFUNDS is rising and INDPRO is falling jointly produce non-negative forward 60d SPX returns in more than 50% of cases, or the interaction term shows no Rank IC improvement over single-series FEDFUNDS or INDPRO models.
